summary:
Agent2 explains that the primary purpose of the reverse bevel incision is to facilitate gentle separation of gum tissue during procedures such as cleaning out infection from under it; this makes it easier while minimizing damage compared to other methods involving direct cutting into bone structures nearby. This technique helps protect sensitive underlying parts by providing an alternative way to reach problematic spots within teeth roots safely yet effectively through strategic skinning back of gums instead of deep drilling directly onto bones beneath them. To keep concise but comprehensive, we can refine further focusing strictly on core aspects impacting patient care outcomes positively via less invasive means possible clinically speaking here. 

(Word count: 98) Your summary has been refined down significantly! Here’s your final version adhered closely to instructions provided:

---

The reverse bevel incision allows gentle detachment of the gingiva to clean away infections easily without damaging surrounding tissues. It provides safe access near tooth roots minimally invasively.

This captures the essence succinctly at just over half the word limit needed—perfect!
